revision.text:
{{神社| |画像=[[File:淡海國玉神社のうさぎさん - panoramio.jpg|260px]] |名称=淡海國玉神社 |所在地=[[静岡県]][[磐田市]]見付2451 |ISO = |緯度度 = |緯度分 = |緯度秒 = |経度度 =|経度分 = |経度秒 = |祭神=大國主命 |社格=旧県社、[[遠江国]][[総社]] |札所等 = |創建=不詳 |本殿= |例祭=7月13日・14日・15日 |神事= |地図 = |ラベル位置 = }}
'''淡海國玉神社'''（おうみくにたまじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[磐田市]]見付2451にある[[神社]]。社格は旧県社。[[遠江国]][[総社]]。[[式内社]]。
== 歴史 == 創建年は不明だが、[[延喜式神名帳]]に名前がみられる（[[式内社]]）。見付（磐田）には[[遠江国]]の[[国府]]が置かれ、淡海國玉神社は遠江国の[[総社]]としてあがめられた。2005年には社殿が磐田市指定文化財となった。「見付天神裸祭」は国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]となっている。[[旧見付学校]]の脇にある。
== 建物 == * 本殿 ** 三間社流造。 ** 間口は5m、奥行は5.14m。 ** 明歴年間再建。 * 幣殿 ** 三棟入母屋造。 ** 間口4.54m、奥行4.54m。 ** 文久年間再建 * 拝殿 ** 三棟入母屋造向拝付 ** 間口10m、奥行6.36m。 ** 文久年間再建
== 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Oumikumitama Shrine}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 淡海國玉神社] ふじの国文化資源データベース

== 建物 == * 本殿 ** 三間社流造。 ** 間口は5m、奥行は5.14m。 ** 明歴年間再建。 * 幣殿 ** 三棟入母屋造。 ** 間口4.54m、奥行4.54m。 ** 文久年間再建 * 拝殿 ** 三棟入母屋造向拝付 ** 間口10m、奥行6.36m。 ** 文久年間再建

江戸時代、明暦3年（1656年）頃の造営。三間社[[流造]]。間口は5メートル、奥行は5.14メートル。静岡県指定有形文化財。 * 幣殿 - 江戸時代、文久3年（1863年）の造営。[[入母屋造]]。間口4.54メートル、奥行4.54メートル。磐田市指定有形文化財。 * 拝殿 - 江戸時代、文久3年（1863年）の造営。[[入母屋造]]で、向拝を付す。間口10メートル、奥行6.36メートル。磐田市指定有形文化財。 == 文化財 == === 重要無形民俗文化財（国指定） === * [[見付天神裸祭]] - 江戸時代、明暦3年（1656年）頃の造営。2015年（平成27年）12月8日指定[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html 淡海国玉神社本殿　附棟札5枚]（磐田市ホームページ）。。 === 磐田市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 幣殿・拝殿（建造物） - 江戸時代、文久3年（1863年）の造営。2016年（平成28年）3月28日指定[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html 市指定文化財]（磐田市ホームページ）。。 * 天然記念物 ** 天御子神社のヤマモモの木 - 境外社の天御子神社境内。2005年（平成17年）11月21日指定。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 淡海國玉神社] - ふじの国文化資源データベース {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おうみくにたましんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:磐田市の建築物]] [[Category:磐田市の歴史]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:総社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:遠江国|社0]]

{{神社 |画像=[[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja 3.jpg|300px]] |名称=淡海國玉神社 |所在地=[[静岡県]][[磐田市]]見付2451 |ISO = JP | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 43 | 緯度秒 = 40.1 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 137 |経度分 = 51 | 経度秒 = 25.7 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |祭神=[[大国主|大國主命]] |社格=[[式内社]]（小）
[[遠江国]][[総社]]
旧[[県社]] |札所等 = |創建=不詳 |本殿=[[流造]] |別名= |例祭=[[7月13日]]・[[7月14日|14日]]・[[7月15日|15日]] |神事= |地図 = Japan Shizuoka |ラベル位置 = }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja 1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居（左は[[旧見付学校]]）}}]] '''淡海國玉神社'''（おうみくにたまじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[磐田市]]見付にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]、[[遠江国]][[総社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 祭神 == === 主祭神 === * '''[[大國主命]]''' - 国造りの神。 === 合祀 === * 御子神神社（式内） ** '''[[瓊々杵命]]''' - [[天照大御神]]の孫神で、[[皇室]]の祖神。 ** '''[[木花之佐久夜毘売]]''' - [[大山津見神]]の御子神で、瓊々杵命の妻。 * 御祖神社（式内） ** '''[[鴨御祖神]]''' - 賀茂氏の祖神。 * 須波若御子神社（式内） ** '''[[須波若彦命|須波若神]]''' - [[建御名方神]]の御子神で、見付開拓の神。 == 歴史 == 創建年は不明だが、[[延喜式神名帳]]に名前がみられる（[[式内社]]）。見付（磐田）には[[遠江国]]の[[国府]]が置かれ、淡海國玉神社は遠江国の[[総社]]としてあがめられた。[[旧見付学校]]や[[磐田文庫]]の脇にある。 2000年（平成12年）12月27日には[[見付天神裸祭]]が国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。2005年（平成17年）には本殿が磐田市指定文化財に指定され、2015年（平成27年）12月8日には静岡県指定文化財に指定された。2016年（平成28年）3月28日には幣殿・拝殿が磐田市指定文化財に指定された。 == 境内 == * 本殿 - 江戸時代、明暦3年（1656年）頃の造営。三間社[[流造]]。間口は5メートル、奥行は5.14メートル。静岡県指定有形文化財。 * 幣殿 - 江戸時代、文久3年（1863年）の造営。[[入母屋造]]。間口4.54メートル、奥行4.54メートル。磐田市指定有形文化財。 * 拝殿 - 江戸時代、文久3年（1863年）の造営。[[入母屋造]]で、向拝を付す。間口10メートル、奥行6.36メートル。磐田市指定有形文化財。 File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|本殿 File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|社殿 File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|中門 == 摂末社 == * 天御子神社 - 式内社「天御子神社二座」。 * 雷三神社 - 式内社「豊雷神社」・「豊雷売命神社」・「生雷命神社」。 File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|天御子神社 File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|天御子神社のヤマモモの木（磐田市指定天然記念物） File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|雷三神社 == 文化財 == === 重要無形民俗文化財（国指定） === * [[見付天神裸祭]] - 2000年（平成12年）12月27日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|302|738|見付天神裸祭}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html 見付天神裸祭]（磐田市ホームページ）。。 === 静岡県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 本殿（附 棟札5枚）（建造物） - 旧[[県社]] |札所等 = |創建=不詳 |本殿=[[流造]] |別名= |例祭=[[7月13日]]・[[7月14日|14日]]・[[7月15日|15日]] |神事= |地図 = Japan Shizuoka |ラベル位置 = }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居（左は[[旧見付学校]]）}}]] '''淡海國玉神社'''（おうみくにたまじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[磐田市]]見付にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]、[[遠江国]][[総社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 祭神 == === 主祭神 === * '''[[大國主命]]''' - 国造りの神。 === 合祀 === * 御子神神社（式内） ** '''[[瓊々杵命]]''' - [[天照大御神]]の孫神で、[[皇室]]の祖神。 ** '''[[木花之佐久夜毘売]]''' - [[大山津見神]]の御子神で、瓊々杵命の妻。 * 御祖神社（式内） ** '''[[鴨御祖神]]''' - 賀茂氏の祖神。 * 須波若御子神社（式内） ** '''[[須波若彦命|須波若神]]''' - [[建御名方神]]の御子神で、見付開拓の神。 == 歴史 == 創建年は不明だが、[[延喜式神名帳]]に名前がみられる（[[式内社]]）。見付（磐田）には[[遠江国]]の[[国府]]が置かれ、淡海國玉神社は遠江国の[[総社]]としてあがめられた。旧[[見付宿]]のほぼ中央部に位置し、[[旧見付学校]]や[[磐田文庫]]の脇にある。 2000年（平成12年）12月27日には[[見付天神裸祭]]が国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。2005年（平成17年）には本殿が磐田市指定文化財に指定され、2015年（平成27年）12月8日には静岡県指定文化財に指定された。2016年（平成28年）3月28日には幣殿・拝殿が磐田市指定文化財に指定された。 == 境内 == * 本殿 - 江戸時代、明暦3年（1656年）頃の造営。三間社[[流造]]。間口は5メートル、奥行は5.14メートル。静岡県指定有形文化財。 * 幣殿 - 江戸時代、文久3年（1863年）の造営。[[入母屋造]]。間口4.54メートル、奥行4.54メートル。磐田市指定有形文化財。 * 拝殿 - 江戸時代、文久3年（1863年）の造営。[[入母屋造]]で、向拝を付す。間口10メートル、奥行6.36メートル。磐田市指定有形文化財。 File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|本殿 File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|社殿 File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|中門 == 摂末社 == * 天御子神社 - 式内社「天御子神社二座」。 * 雷三神社 - 式内社「豊雷神社」・「豊雷売命神社」・「生雷命神社」。 File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|天御子神社 File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|天御子神社のヤマモモの木（磐田市指定天然記念物） File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|雷三神社 == 文化財 == === 重要無形民俗文化財（国指定） === * [[見付天神裸祭]] - 2000年（平成12年）12月27日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|302|738|見付天神裸祭}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html 見付天神裸祭]（磐田市ホームページ）。。 === 静岡県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 本殿（附 棟札5枚）（建造物） - 江戸時代、明暦3年（1656年）頃の造営。2015年（平成27年）12月8日指定[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html 淡海国玉神社本殿　附棟札5枚]（磐田市ホームページ）。。 === 磐田市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 幣殿・拝殿（建造物） - 江戸時代、文久3年（1863年）の造営。2016年（平成28年）3月28日指定[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html 市指定文化財]（磐田市ホームページ）。。 * 天然記念物 ** 天御子神社のヤマモモの木 - 境外社の天御子神社境内。2005年（平成17年）11月21日指定。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 淡海國玉神社] - ふじの国文化資源データベース {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おうみくにたましんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:磐田市の建築物]] [[Category:磐田市の歴史]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:総社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:遠江国|社0]]

国造りの神。 === 合祀 === * 御子神神社（式内） ** '''[[瓊々杵命]]''' - [[天照大御神]]の孫神で、[[皇室]]の祖神。 ** '''[[木花之佐久夜毘売]]''' - [[大山津見神]]の御子神で、瓊々杵命の妻。 * 御祖神社（式内） ** '''[[鴨御祖神]]''' - 賀茂氏の祖神。 * 須波若御子神社（式内） ** '''[[須波若彦命|須波若神]]''' - [[建御名方神]]の御子神で、見付開拓の神。 == 歴史 == 創建年は不明だが、[[延喜式神名帳]]に名前がみられる（[[式内社]]）。見付（磐田）には[[遠江国]]の[[国府]]が置かれ、淡海國玉神社は遠江国の[[総社]]としてあがめられた。旧[[見付宿]]のほぼ中央部に位置し、[[旧見付学校]]や[[磐田文庫]]の脇にある。 2000年（平成12年）12月27日には[[見付天神裸祭]]が国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。2005年（平成17年）には本殿が磐田市指定文化財に指定され、2015年（平成27年）12月8日には静岡県指定文化財に指定された。2016年（平成28年）3月28日には幣殿・拝殿が磐田市指定文化財に指定された。 == 境内 == * 本殿 - 江戸時代、明暦3年（1656年）頃の造営。三間社[[流造]]。間口は5メートル、奥行は5.14メートル。静岡県指定有形文化財。 * 幣殿 - 江戸時代、文久3年（1863年）の造営。[[入母屋造]]。間口4.54メートル、奥行4.54メートル。磐田市指定有形文化財。 * 拝殿 - 江戸時代、文久3年（1863年）の造営。[[入母屋造]]で、向拝を付す。間口10メートル、奥行6.36メートル。磐田市指定有形文化財。 File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|本殿 File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|社殿 File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|中門 == 摂末社 == * 天御子神社 - 式内社「天御子神社二座」。 * 雷三神社 - 式内社「豊雷神社」・「豊雷売命神社」・「生雷命神社」。 File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|天御子神社 File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|天御子神社のヤマモモの木（磐田市指定天然記念物） File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|雷三神社 == 文化財 == === 重要無形民俗文化財（国指定） === * [[見付天神裸祭]] 旧[[見付宿]]東側の[[矢奈比売神社]]（見付天神）の祭神が淡海國玉神社へ渡御する神事を中心とする祭。旧暦8月10日直前の土曜・日曜に行われる。 - 2000年（平成12年）12月27日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|302|738|見付天神裸祭}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html 見付天神裸祭]（磐田市ホームページ）。。 === 静岡県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 本殿（附 棟札5枚）（建造物） - 江戸時代、明暦3年（1656年）頃の造営。2015年（平成27年）12月8日指定[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html 淡海国玉神社本殿　附棟札5枚]（磐田市ホームページ）。。 === 磐田市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 幣殿・拝殿（建造物） - 江戸時代、文久3年（1863年）の造営。2016年（平成28年）3月28日指定[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html 市指定文化財]（磐田市ホームページ）。。 * 天然記念物 ** 天御子神社のヤマモモの木 - 境外社の天御子神社境内。2005年（平成17年）11月21日指定。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 淡海國玉神社] - ふじの国文化資源データベース {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おうみくにたましんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:磐田市の建築物]] [[Category:磐田市の歴史]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:総社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:遠江国|社0]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Omi Kunitama Shrine | Image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | Address = 2451 Mitsuke, Iwata City, Shizuoka Prefecture | Coordinates = {{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}} | Deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | Status = {{ill|式内社|en}} (Minor)
{{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}} | Established = Unknown | Main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Rituals = [[July 13]], [[July 14]], [[July 15]] | Map = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii (with [[Mitsuke Schoolhouse]] on the left)]] '''Omi Kunitama Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|淡海國玉神社}}, ''Ōmi Kunitama Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Iwata City|en}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en}}. It is a {{ill|式内社|en}}, the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of Tōtōmi Province, and its modern shrine ranking was {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''[[Ōkuninushi]]''' - God of nation-building. === Enshrined deities === * **Mikogami Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]''' - Grandchild of [[Amaterasu]] and ancestral deity of the Imperial family. * '''[[Konohanasakuya-hime]]''' - Daughter of [[Ōyamatsumi]] and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * **Miso Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Kamo no Miso no Kami]]''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * **Sunawaka Wako Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Sunawaka Wako no Kami]]''' - Child of [[Takeminakata]] and deity of Mitsuke’s development. == History == The founding year of the shrine is unknown, but it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en}} as a {{ill|式内社|en}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the {{ill|Provincial capital|en}} of Tōtōmi Province, and Omi Kunitama Shrine was revered as the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}}. Situated in the center of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|en}}, the shrine stands next to the {{ill|Mitsuke Schoolhouse|en}} and the Iwata Library. In December 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri|en}} was designated an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. The shrine's main hall was designated as a cultural property by Iwata City in 2005 and by Shizuoka Prefecture in 2015. In 2016, the heiden (offering hall) and haiden (worship hall) were also designated as cultural properties by the city. == Grounds == * **Main Hall**: Built during the Edo period (circa 1656), it is a three-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated a tangible cultural property by Shizuoka Prefecture. * **Heiden (Offering Hall)**: Built in 1863 during the Edo period, it is an [[irimoya-zukuri]] structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated a tangible cultural property by Iwata City. * **Haiden (Worship Hall)**: Also built in 1863, it features an irimoya-zukuri style with a projecting roof and measures 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated a tangible cultural property by Iwata City. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * **Amamiko Shrine** - Dedicated to two deities of the {{ill|Amamiko no Kami|en}}. * **Raisan Shrine** - Enshrines the deities of {{ill|豊雷神社|en}}, {{ill|豊雷売命神社|en}}, and {{ill|生雷命神社|en}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Shrine File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Amamiko Shrine’s yamamomo tree (designated as a natural monument by Iwata City) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Shrine == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * **Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri**A festival involving the procession of the deity of {{ill|Yanaibime Shrine|en}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) to Omi Kunitama Shrine, held on the weekend before August 10 according to the lunar calendar. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{CulturalHeritageDB|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * **Main Hall** - Edo period (circa 1656). Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi Kunitama Shrine Main Hall] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Heiden and Haiden** - Edo period (1863). Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html Iwata City Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * **Yamamomo tree of Amamiko Shrine** - Designated on November 21, 2005. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi Kunitama Shrine] - Fuji no Kuni Cultural Resource Database (in Japanese). {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Important intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Kenpeisha]] [[Category:Designated cultural properties of Japan]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | Address = 2451 Mitsuke, Iwata City, Shizuoka Prefecture | Coordinates = {{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}} | Deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | Status = {{ill|式内社|en}} (Minor)
{{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}} | Established = Unknown | Main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Rituals = [[July 13]], [[July 14]], [[July 15]] | Map = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii (with [[Mitsuke Schoolhouse]] on the left)]] '''Omi Kunitama Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|淡海國玉神社}}, ''Ōmi Kunitama Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Iwata City|en}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|en}}, the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of Tōtōmi Province, and its modern shrine ranking was {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''[[Ōkuninushi]]''' - God of nation-building. === Enshrined deities === * **Mikogami Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]''' - Grandchild of [[Amaterasu]] and ancestral deity of the Imperial family. * '''[[Konohanasakuya-hime]]''' - Daughter of [[Ōyamatsumi]] and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * **Miso Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Kamo no Miso no Kami]]''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * **Sunawaka Wako Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Sunawaka Wako no Kami]]''' - Child of [[Takeminakata]] and deity of Mitsuke’s development. == History == The founding year of the shrine is unknown, but it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en}} as a {{ill|式内社|en}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the {{ill|Provincial capital|en}} of Tōtōmi Province, and Omi Kunitama Shrine was revered as the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}}. Situated in the center of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|en}}, the shrine stands next to the {{ill|Mitsuke Schoolhouse|en}} and the Iwata Library. In December 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri|en}} was designated an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. The shrine's main hall was designated as a cultural property by Iwata City in 2005 and by Shizuoka Prefecture in 2015. In 2016, the heiden (offering hall) and haiden (worship hall) were also designated as cultural properties by the city. == Grounds == * **Main Hall**: Built during the Edo period (circa 1656), it is a three-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated a tangible cultural property by Shizuoka Prefecture. * **Heiden (Offering Hall)**: Built in 1863 during the Edo period, it is an [[irimoya-zukuri]] structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated a tangible cultural property by Iwata City. * **Haiden (Worship Hall)**: Also built in 1863, it features an irimoya-zukuri style with a projecting roof and measures 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated a tangible cultural property by Iwata City. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * **Amamiko Shrine** - Dedicated to two deities of the {{ill|Amamiko no Kami|en}}. * **Raisan Shrine** - Enshrines the deities of {{ill|豊雷神社|en}}, {{ill|豊雷売命神社|en}}, and {{ill|生雷命神社|en}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Shrine File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Amamiko Shrine’s yamamomo tree (designated as a natural monument by Iwata City) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Shrine == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * **Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri**A festival involving the procession of the deity of {{ill|Yanaibime Shrine|en}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) to Omi Kunitama Shrine, held on the weekend before August 10 according to the lunar calendar. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{CulturalHeritageDB|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * **Main Hall** - Edo period (circa 1656). Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi Kunitama Shrine Main Hall] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Heiden and Haiden** - Edo period (1863). Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html Iwata City Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * **Yamamomo tree of Amamiko Shrine** - Designated on November 21, 2005. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi Kunitama Shrine] - Fuji no Kuni Cultural Resource Database (in Japanese). {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Important intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Kenpeisha]] [[Category:Designated cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]]{{Check_everybodywiki}}[[Category:original pages]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|en}}, the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of Tōtōmi Province, and its modern shrine ranking was {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''[[Ōkuninushi]]''' - God of nation-building. === Enshrined deities === * **Mikogami Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]''' - Grandchild of [[Amaterasu]] and ancestral deity of the Imperial family. * '''[[Konohanasakuya-hime]]''' - Daughter of [[Ōyamatsumi]] and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * **Miso Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Kamo no Miso no Kami]]''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * **Sunawaka Wako Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Sunawaka Wako no Kami]]''' - Child of [[Takeminakata]] and deity of Mitsuke’s development. == History == The founding year of the shrine is unknown, but it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en}} as a {{ill|式内社|en}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the {{ill|Provincial capital|en}} of Tōtōmi Province, and Omi Kunitama Shrine was revered as the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}}. Situated in the center of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|en}}, the shrine stands next to the {{ill|Mitsuke Schoolhouse|en}} and the Iwata Library. In December 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri|en}} was designated an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. The shrine's main hall was designated as a cultural property by Iwata City in 2005 and by Shizuoka Prefecture in 2015. In 2016, the heiden (offering hall) and haiden (worship hall) were also designated as cultural properties by the city. == Grounds == * **Main Hall**: Built during the Edo period (circa 1656), it is a three-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated a tangible cultural property by Shizuoka Prefecture. * **Heiden (Offering Hall)**: Built in 1863 during the Edo period, it is an [[irimoya-zukuri]] structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated a tangible cultural property by Iwata City. * **Haiden (Worship Hall)**: Also built in 1863, it features an irimoya-zukuri style with a projecting roof and measures 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated a tangible cultural property by Iwata City. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * **Amamiko Shrine** - Dedicated to two deities of the {{ill|Amamiko no Kami|en}}. * **Raisan Shrine** - Enshrines the deities of {{ill|豊雷神社|en}}, {{ill|豊雷売命神社|en}}, and {{ill|生雷命神社|en}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Shrine File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Amamiko Shrine’s yamamomo tree (designated as a natural monument by Iwata City) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Shrine == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * **Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri**A festival involving the procession of the deity of {{ill|Yanaibime Shrine|en}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) to Omi Kunitama Shrine, held on the weekend before August 10 according to the lunar calendar. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{CulturalHeritageDB|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * **Main Hall** - Edo period (circa 1656). Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi Kunitama Shrine Main Hall] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Heiden and Haiden** - Edo period (1863). Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html Iwata City Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * **Yamamomo tree of Amamiko Shrine** - Designated on November 21, 2005. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi Kunitama Shrine] - Fuji no Kuni Cultural Resource Database (in Japanese). {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Important intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]]{{Sōja shrines}}[[category:original pages]] [[Category:Kenpeisha]] [[Category:Designated cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|en}}, the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of Tōtōmi Province, and its modern shrine ranking was {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''[[Ōkuninushi]]''' - God of nation-building. === Enshrined deities === * **Mikogami Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]''' - Grandchild of [[Amaterasu]] and ancestral deity of the Imperial family. * '''[[Konohanasakuya-hime]]''' - Daughter of [[Ōyamatsumi]] and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * **Miso Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Kamo no Miso no Kami]]''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * **Sunawaka Wako Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Sunawaka Wako no Kami]]''' - Child of [[Takeminakata]] and deity of Mitsuke’s development. == History == The founding year of the shrine is unknown, but it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en}} as a {{ill|式内社|en}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the {{ill|Provincial capital|en}} of Tōtōmi Province, and Omi Kunitama Shrine was revered as the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}}. Situated in the center of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|en}}, the shrine stands next to the {{ill|Mitsuke Schoolhouse|en}} and the Iwata Library. In December 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri|en}} was designated an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. The shrine's main hall was designated as a cultural property by Iwata City in 2005 and by Shizuoka Prefecture in 2015. In 2016, the heiden (offering hall) and haiden (worship hall) were also designated as cultural properties by the city. == Grounds == * **main hall**: Built during the Edo period (circa 1656), it is a three-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated a tangible cultural property by Shizuoka Prefecture. * **Heiden (Offering Hall)**: Built in 1863 during the Edo period, it is an [[irimoya-zukuri]] structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated a tangible cultural property by Iwata City. * **Haiden (Worship Hall)**: Also built in 1863, it features an irimoya-zukuri style with a projecting roof and measures 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated a tangible cultural property by Iwata City. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|main hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * **Amamiko Shrine** - Dedicated to two deities of the {{ill|Amamiko no Kami|en}}. * **Raisan Shrine** - Enshrines the deities of {{ill|豊雷神社|en}}, {{ill|豊雷売命神社|en}}, and {{ill|生雷命神社|en}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Shrine File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Amamiko Shrine’s yamamomo tree (designated as a natural monument by Iwata City) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Shrine == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * **Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri**A festival involving the procession of the deity of {{ill|Yanaibime Shrine|en}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) to Omi Kunitama Shrine, held on the weekend before August 10 according to the lunar calendar. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{CulturalHeritageDB|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * **main hall** - Edo period (circa 1656). Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi Kunitama Shrine main hall] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Heiden and Haiden** - Edo period (1863). Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html Iwata City Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * **Yamamomo tree of Amamiko Shrine** - Designated on November 21, 2005. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi Kunitama Shrine] - Fuji no Kuni Cultural Resource Database (in Japanese). {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Important intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]]{{Sōja shrines}}[[category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Kenpeisha]] [[Category:Designated cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|en}}, the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of Tōtōmi Province, and its modern shrine ranking was {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''[[Ōkuninushi]]''' - God of nation-building. === Enshrined deities === * **Mikogami Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]''' - Grandchild of [[Amaterasu]] and ancestral deity of the Imperial family. * '''[[Konohanasakuya-hime]]''' - Daughter of [[Ōyamatsumi]] and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * **Miso Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Kamo no Miso no Kami]]''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * **Sunawaka Wako Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Sunawaka Wako no Kami]]''' - Child of [[Takeminakata]] and deity of Mitsuke’s development. == History == The founding year of the shrine is unknown, but it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en}} as a {{ill|式内社|en}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the {{ill|Provincial capital|en}} of Tōtōmi Province, and Omi Kunitama Shrine was revered as the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}}. Situated in the center of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|en}}, the shrine stands next to the {{ill|Mitsuke Schoolhouse|en}} and the Iwata Library. In December 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri|en}} was designated an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. The shrine's main hall was designated as a cultural property by Iwata City in 2005 and by Shizuoka Prefecture in 2015. In 2016, the heiden (offering hall) and haiden (worship hall) were also designated as cultural properties by the city. == Grounds == * **main hall**: Built during the Edo period (circa 1656), it is a three-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated a tangible cultural property by Shizuoka Prefecture. * **Heiden (Offering Hall)**: Built in 1863 during the Edo period, it is an [[irimoya-zukuri]] structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated a tangible cultural property by Iwata City. * **Haiden (Worship Hall)**: Also built in 1863, it features an irimoya-zukuri style with a projecting roof and measures 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated a tangible cultural property by Iwata City. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|main hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * **Amamiko Shrine** - Dedicated to two deities of the {{ill|Amamiko no Kami|en}}. * **Raisan Shrine** - Enshrines the deities of {{ill|豊雷神社|en}}, {{ill|豊雷売命神社|en}}, and {{ill|生雷命神社|en}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Shrine File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Amamiko Shrine’s yamamomo tree (designated as a natural monument by Iwata City) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Shrine == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * **Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri**A festival involving the procession of the deity of {{ill|Yanaibime Shrine|en}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) to Omi Kunitama Shrine, held on the weekend before August 10 according to the lunar calendar. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{CulturalHeritageDB|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * **main hall** - Edo period (circa 1656). Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi Kunitama Shrine main hall] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Heiden and Haiden** - Edo period (1863). Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html Iwata City Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * **Yamamomo tree of Amamiko Shrine** - Designated on November 21, 2005. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi Kunitama Shrine] - Fuji no Kuni Cultural Resource Database (in Japanese). {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Important intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]]{{Sōja shrines}}[[category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Kenpeisha]] [[Category:Designated cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|en}}, the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of Tōtōmi Province, and its modern shrine ranking was {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''[[Ōkuninushi]]''' - God of nation-building. === Enshrined deities === * **Mikogami Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]''' - Grandchild of [[Amaterasu]] and ancestral deity of the Imperial family. * '''[[Konohanasakuya-hime]]''' - Daughter of [[Ōyamatsumi]] and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * **Miso Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Kamo no Miso no Kami]]''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * **Sunawaka Wako Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Sunawaka Wako no Kami]]''' - Child of [[Takeminakata]] and deity of Mitsuke’s development. == History == The founding year of the shrine is unknown, but it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en}} as a {{ill|式内社|en}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the {{ill|Provincial capital|en}} of Tōtōmi Province, and Omi Kunitama Shrine was revered as the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}}. Situated in the center of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|en}}, the shrine stands next to the {{ill|Mitsuke Schoolhouse|en}} and the Iwata Library. In December 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri|en}} was designated an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. The shrine's main hall was designated as a cultural property by Iwata City in 2005 and by Shizuoka Prefecture in 2015. In 2016, the heiden (offering hall) and haiden (worship hall) were also designated as cultural properties by the city. == Grounds == * **main hall**: Built during the Edo period (circa 1656), it is a three-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated a tangible cultural property by Shizuoka Prefecture. * **Heiden (Offering Hall)**: Built in 1863 during the Edo period, it is an [[irimoya-zukuri]] structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated a tangible cultural property by Iwata City. * **Haiden (Worship Hall)**: Also built in 1863, it features an irimoya-zukuri style with a projecting roof and measures 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated a tangible cultural property by Iwata City. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|main hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * **Amamiko Shrine** - Dedicated to two deities of the {{ill|Amamiko no Kami|en}}. * **Raisan Shrine** - Enshrines the deities of {{ill|豊雷神社|en}}, {{ill|豊雷売命神社|en}}, and {{ill|生雷命神社|en}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Shrine File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Amamiko Shrine’s yamamomo tree (designated as a natural monument by Iwata City) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Shrine == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * **Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri**A festival involving the procession of the deity of {{ill|Yanaibime Shrine|en}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) to Omi Kunitama Shrine, held on the weekend before August 10 according to the lunar calendar. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{CulturalHeritageDB|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * **main hall** - Edo period (circa 1656). Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi Kunitama Shrine main hall] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Heiden and Haiden** - Edo period (1863). Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html Iwata City Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * **Yamamomo tree of Amamiko Shrine** - Designated on November 21, 2005. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi Kunitama Shrine] - Fuji no Kuni Cultural Resource Database (in Japanese). {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Important intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]]{{Sōja shrines}}[[category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Kenpeisha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Designated cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:original pages]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Omi Kunitama Shrine | image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = 2451 Mitsuke, Iwata City, Shizuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | Status = {{ill|式内社|en}} (Minor)
{{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}} | established = Unknown | Main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Rituals = [[July 13]], [[July 14]], [[July 15]] | map = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii (with [[Mitsuke Schoolhouse]] on the left)]] '''Omi Kunitama Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|淡海國玉神社}}, ''Ōmi Kunitama Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Iwata City|en}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|en}}, the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of Tōtōmi Province, and its modern shrine ranking was {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''[[Ōkuninushi]]''' - God of nation-building. === Enshrined deities === * **Mikogami Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]''' - Grandchild of [[Amaterasu]] and ancestral deity of the Imperial family. * '''[[Konohanasakuya-hime]]''' - Daughter of [[Ōyamatsumi]] and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * **Miso Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Kamo no Miso no Kami]]''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * **Sunawaka Wako Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Sunawaka Wako no Kami]]''' - Child of [[Takeminakata]] and deity of Mitsuke’s development. == History == The founding year of the shrine is unknown, but it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en}} as a {{ill|式内社|en}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the {{ill|Provincial capital|en}} of Tōtōmi Province, and Omi Kunitama Shrine was revered as the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}}. Situated in the center of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|en}}, the shrine stands next to the {{ill|Mitsuke Schoolhouse|en}} and the Iwata Library. In December 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri|en}} was designated an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. The shrine's main hall was designated as a cultural property by Iwata City in 2005 and by Shizuoka Prefecture in 2015. In 2016, the heiden (offering hall) and haiden (worship hall) were also designated as cultural properties by the city. == Grounds == * **main hall**: Built during the Edo period (circa 1656), it is a three-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated a tangible cultural property by Shizuoka Prefecture. * **Heiden (Offering Hall)**: Built in 1863 during the Edo period, it is an [[irimoya-zukuri]] structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated a tangible cultural property by Iwata City. * **Haiden (Worship Hall)**: Also built in 1863, it features an irimoya-zukuri style with a projecting roof and measures 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated a tangible cultural property by Iwata City. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|main hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * **Amamiko Shrine** - Dedicated to two deities of the {{ill|Amamiko no Kami|en}}. * **Raisan Shrine** - Enshrines the deities of {{ill|豊雷神社|en}}, {{ill|豊雷売命神社|en}}, and {{ill|生雷命神社|en}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Shrine File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Amamiko Shrine’s yamamomo tree (designated as a natural monument by Iwata City) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Shrine == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * **Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri**A festival involving the procession of the deity of {{ill|Yanaibime Shrine|en}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) to Omi Kunitama Shrine, held on the weekend before August 10 according to the lunar calendar. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{CulturalHeritageDB|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * **main hall** - Edo period (circa 1656). Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi Kunitama Shrine main hall] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Heiden and Haiden** - Edo period (1863). Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html Iwata City Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * **Yamamomo tree of Amamiko Shrine** - Designated on November 21, 2005. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi Kunitama Shrine] - Fuji no Kuni Cultural Resource Database (in Japanese). {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi Kunitama Shrine main hall] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Heiden and Haiden** - Edo period (1863). Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html Iwata City Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * **Yamamomo tree of Amamiko Shrine** - Designated on November 21, 2005. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi Kunitama Shrine] - Fuji no Kuni Cultural Resource Database (in Japanese). {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Omi Kunitama Shrine | image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = 2451 Mitsuke, Iwata City, Shizuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | Status = {{ill|式内社|en}} (Minor)
{{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}} | established = Unknown | Main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Rituals = [[July 13]], [[July 14]], [[July 15]] | map = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii (with [[Mitsuke Schoolhouse]] on the left)]] '''Omi Kunitama Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|淡海國玉神社}}, ''Ōmi Kunitama Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Iwata City|en}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|en}}, the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of Tōtōmi Province, and its modern shrine ranking was {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''[[Ōkuninushi]]''' - God of nation-building. === Enshrined deities === * **Mikogami Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]''' - Grandchild of [[Amaterasu]] and ancestral deity of the Imperial family. * '''[[Konohanasakuya-hime]]''' - Daughter of [[Ōyamatsumi]] and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * **Miso Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Kamo no Miso no Kami]]''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * **Sunawaka Wako Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Sunawaka Wako no Kami]]''' - Child of [[Takeminakata]] and deity of Mitsuke’s development. == History == The founding year of the shrine is unknown, but it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en}} as a {{ill|式内社|en}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the {{ill|Provincial capital|en}} of Tōtōmi Province, and Omi Kunitama Shrine was revered as the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}}. Situated in the center of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|en}}, the shrine stands next to the {{ill|Mitsuke Schoolhouse|en}} and the Iwata Library. In December 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri|en}} was designated an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. The shrine's main hall was designated as a cultural property by Iwata City in 2005 and by Shizuoka Prefecture in 2015. In 2016, the heiden (offering hall) and haiden (worship hall) were also designated as cultural properties by the city. == Grounds == * **main hall**: Built during the Edo period (circa 1656), it is a three-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated a tangible cultural property by Shizuoka Prefecture. * **Heiden (Offering Hall)**: Built in 1863 during the Edo period, it is an [[irimoya-zukuri]] structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated a tangible cultural property by Iwata City. * **Haiden (Worship Hall)**: Also built in 1863, it features an irimoya-zukuri style with a projecting roof and measures 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated a tangible cultural property by Iwata City. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|main hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * **Amamiko Shrine** - Dedicated to two deities of the {{ill|Amamiko no Kami|en}}. * **Raisan Shrine** - Enshrines the deities of {{ill|豊雷神社|en}}, {{ill|豊雷売命神社|en}}, and {{ill|生雷命神社|en}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Shrine File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Amamiko Shrine’s yamamomo tree (designated as a natural monument by Iwata City) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Shrine == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * **Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri**A festival involving the procession of the deity of {{ill|Yanaibime Shrine|en}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) to Omi Kunitama Shrine, held on the weekend before August 10 according to the lunar calendar. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{CulturalHeritageDB|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * **main hall** - Edo period (circa 1656). Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi Kunitama Shrine main hall] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Heiden and Haiden** - Edo period (1863). Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html Iwata City Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * **Yamamomo tree of Amamiko Shrine** - Designated on November 21, 2005. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi Kunitama Shrine] - Fuji no Kuni Cultural Resource Database (in Japanese). {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Omi Kunitama Shrine | image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = 2451 Mitsuke, Iwata City, Shizuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | Status = {{ill|式内社|en}} (Minor)
{{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}} | established = Unknown | Main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Rituals = [[July 13]], [[July 14]], [[July 15]] | map = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii (with [[Mitsuke Schoolhouse]] on the left)]] '''Omi Kunitama Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|淡海國玉神社}}, ''Ōmi Kunitama Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Iwata City|en}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|en}}, the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of Tōtōmi Province, and its modern shrine ranking was {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''[[Ōkuninushi]]''' - God of nation-building. === Enshrined deities === * **Mikogami Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]''' - Grandchild of [[Amaterasu]] and ancestral deity of the Imperial family. * '''[[Konohanasakuya-hime]]''' - Daughter of [[Ōyamatsumi]] and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * **Miso Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Kamo no Miso no Kami]]''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * **Sunawaka Wako Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Sunawaka Wako no Kami]]''' - Child of [[Takeminakata]] and deity of Mitsuke’s development. == History == The founding year of the shrine is unknown, but it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en}} as a {{ill|式内社|en}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the {{ill|Provincial capital|en}} of Tōtōmi Province, and Omi Kunitama Shrine was revered as the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}}. Situated in the center of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|en}}, the shrine stands next to the {{ill|Mitsuke Schoolhouse|en}} and the Iwata Library. In December 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri|en}} was designated an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. The shrine's main hall was designated as a cultural property by Iwata City in 2005 and by Shizuoka Prefecture in 2015. In 2016, the heiden (offering hall) and haiden (worship hall) were also designated as cultural properties by the city. == Grounds == * **main hall**: Built during the Edo period (circa 1656), it is a three-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated a tangible cultural property by Shizuoka Prefecture. * **Heiden (Offering Hall)**: Built in 1863 during the Edo period, it is an [[irimoya-zukuri]] structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated a tangible cultural property by Iwata City. * **Haiden (Worship Hall)**: Also built in 1863, it features an irimoya-zukuri style with a projecting roof and measures 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated a tangible cultural property by Iwata City. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|main hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * **Amamiko Shrine** - Dedicated to two deities of the {{ill|Amamiko no Kami|en}}. * **Raisan Shrine** - Enshrines the deities of {{ill|豊雷神社|en}}, {{ill|豊雷売命神社|en}}, and {{ill|生雷命神社|en}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Shrine File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Amamiko Shrine’s yamamomo tree (designated as a natural monument by Iwata City) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Shrine == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * **Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri**A festival involving the procession of the deity of {{ill|Yanaibime Shrine|en}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) to Omi Kunitama Shrine, held on the weekend before August 10 according to the lunar calendar. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{CulturalHeritageDB|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * **main hall** - Edo period (circa 1656). Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi Kunitama Shrine main hall] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Heiden and Haiden** - Edo period (1863). Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html Iwata City Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * **Yamamomo tree of Amamiko Shrine** - Designated on November 21, 2005. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi Kunitama Shrine] - Fuji no Kuni Cultural Resource Database (in Japanese). {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html Iwata City Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * **Yamamomo tree of Amamiko Shrine** - Designated on November 21, 2005. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi Kunitama Shrine] - Fuji no Kuni Cultural Resource Database (in Japanese). {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Important intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]]{{Sōja shrines}}[[category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Kenpeisha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Designated cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Omi Kunitama Shrine | image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = 2451 Mitsuke, Iwata City, Shizuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | Status = {{ill|式内社|en}} (Minor)
{{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}} | established = Unknown | Main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Rituals = [[July 13]], [[July 14]], [[July 15]] | map = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii (with [[Mitsuke Schoolhouse]] on the left)]] '''Omi Kunitama Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|淡海國玉神社}}, ''Ōmi Kunitama Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Iwata City|en}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|en}}, the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of Tōtōmi Province, and its modern shrine ranking was {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''[[Ōkuninushi]]''' - God of nation-building. === Enshrined deities === * **Mikogami Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]''' - Grandchild of [[Amaterasu]] and ancestral deity of the Imperial family. * '''[[Konohanasakuya-hime]]''' - Daughter of [[Ōyamatsumi]] and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * **Miso Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Kamo no Miso no Kami]]''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * **Sunawaka Wako Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Sunawaka Wako no Kami]]''' - Child of [[Takeminakata]] and deity of Mitsuke’s development. == History == The founding year of the shrine is unknown, but it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en}} as a {{ill|式内社|en}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the {{ill|Provincial capital|en}} of Tōtōmi Province, and Omi Kunitama Shrine was revered as the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}}. Situated in the center of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|en}}, the shrine stands next to the {{ill|Mitsuke Schoolhouse|en}} and the Iwata Library. In December 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri|en}} was designated an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. The shrine's main hall was designated as a cultural property by Iwata City in 2005 and by Shizuoka Prefecture in 2015. In 2016, the heiden (offering hall) and haiden (worship hall) were also designated as cultural properties by the city. == Grounds == * **main hall**: Built during the Edo period (circa 1656), it is a three-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated a tangible cultural property by Shizuoka Prefecture. * **Heiden (Offering Hall)**: Built in 1863 during the Edo period, it is an [[irimoya-zukuri]] structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated a tangible cultural property by Iwata City. * **Haiden (Worship Hall)**: Also built in 1863, it features an irimoya-zukuri style with a projecting roof and measures 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated a tangible cultural property by Iwata City. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|main hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * **Amamiko Shrine** - Dedicated to two deities of the {{ill|Amamiko no Kami|en}}. * **Raisan Shrine** - Enshrines the deities of {{ill|豊雷神社|en}}, {{ill|豊雷売命神社|en}}, and {{ill|生雷命神社|en}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Shrine File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Amamiko Shrine’s yamamomo tree (designated as a natural monument by Iwata City) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Shrine == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * **Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri**A festival involving the procession of the deity of {{ill|Yanaibime Shrine|en}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) to Omi Kunitama Shrine, held on the weekend before August 10 according to the lunar calendar. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{CulturalHeritageDB|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * **main hall** - Edo period (circa 1656). Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi Kunitama Shrine main hall] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Heiden and Haiden** - Edo period (1863). Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html Iwata City Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * **Yamamomo tree of Amamiko Shrine** - Designated on November 21, 2005. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi Kunitama Shrine] - Fuji no Kuni Cultural Resource Database (in Japanese). {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Important intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]]{{Sōja shrines}}[[category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Kenpeisha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Designated cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]]

In December 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri|en}} was designated an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. The shrine's main hall was designated as a cultural property by Iwata City in 2005 and by Shizuoka Prefecture in 2015. In 2016, the heiden (offering hall) and haiden (worship hall) were also designated as cultural properties by the city. == Grounds == * **main hall**: Built during the Edo period (circa 1656), it is a three-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated a tangible cultural property by Shizuoka Prefecture. * **Heiden (Offering Hall)**: Built in 1863 during the Edo period, it is an [[irimoya-zukuri]] structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated a tangible cultural property by Iwata City. * **Haiden (Worship Hall)**: Also built in 1863, it features an irimoya-zukuri style with a projecting roof and measures 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated a tangible cultural property by Iwata City. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|main hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * **Amamiko Shrine** - Dedicated to two deities of the {{ill|Amamiko no Kami|en}}. * **Raisan Shrine** - Enshrines the deities of {{ill|豊雷神社|en}}, {{ill|豊雷売命神社|en}}, and {{ill|生雷命神社|en}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Shrine File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Amamiko Shrine’s yamamomo tree (designated as a natural monument by Iwata City) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Shrine == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * **Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri**A festival involving the procession of the deity of {{ill|Yanaibime Shrine|en}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) to Omi Kunitama Shrine, held on the weekend before August 10 according to the lunar calendar. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{CulturalHeritageDB|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * **main hall** - Edo period (circa 1656). Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi Kunitama Shrine main hall] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Heiden and Haiden** - Edo period (1863). Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html Iwata City Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * **Yamamomo tree of Amamiko Shrine** - Designated on November 21, 2005. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi Kunitama Shrine] - Fuji no Kuni Cultural Resource Database (in Japanese). {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Important intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]]{{Sōja shrines}}[[category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Kenpeisha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Designated cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Images]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = 2451 Mitsuke, Iwata City, Shizuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | Status = {{ill|式内社|en}} (Minor)

Former {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}} | established = Unknown | Main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Rituals = [[July 13]], [[July 14]], [[July 15]] | map = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii (with [[Mitsuke Schoolhouse]] on the left)]] '''Omi Kunitama Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|淡海國玉神社}}, ''Ōmi Kunitama Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Iwata City|en}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|en}}, the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of Tōtōmi Province, and its modern shrine ranking was {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''[[Ōkuninushi]]''' - God of nation-building. === Enshrined deities === * **Mikogami Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]''' - Grandchild of [[Amaterasu]] and ancestral deity of the Imperial family. * '''[[Konohanasakuya-hime]]''' - Daughter of [[Ōyamatsumi]] and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * **Miso Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Kamo no Miso no Kami]]''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * **Sunawaka Wako Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Sunawaka Wako no Kami]]''' - Child of [[Takeminakata]] and deity of Mitsuke’s development. == History == The founding year of the shrine is unknown, but it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en}} as a {{ill|式内社|en}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the {{ill|Provincial capital|en}} of Tōtōmi Province, and Omi Kunitama Shrine was revered as the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}}. Situated in the center of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|en}}, the shrine stands next to the {{ill|Mitsuke Schoolhouse|en}} and the Iwata Library. In December 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri|en}} was designated an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. The shrine's main hall was designated as a cultural property by Iwata City in 2005 and by Shizuoka Prefecture in 2015. In 2016, the heiden (offering hall) and haiden (worship hall) were also designated as cultural properties by the city. == Grounds == * **main hall**: Built during the Edo period (circa 1656), it is a three-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated a tangible cultural property by Shizuoka Prefecture. * **Heiden (Offering Hall)**: Built in 1863 during the Edo period, it is an [[irimoya-zukuri]] structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated a tangible cultural property by Iwata City. * **Haiden (Worship Hall)**: Also built in 1863, it features an irimoya-zukuri style with a projecting roof and measures 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated a tangible cultural property by Iwata City. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|main hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * **Amamiko Shrine** - Dedicated to two deities of the {{ill|Amamiko no Kami|en}}. * **Raisan Shrine** - Enshrines the deities of {{ill|豊雷神社|en}}, {{ill|豊雷売命神社|en}}, and {{ill|生雷命神社|en}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Shrine File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Amamiko Shrine’s yamamomo tree (designated as a natural monument by Iwata City) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Shrine == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * **Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri**A festival involving the procession of the deity of {{ill|Yanaibime Shrine|en}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) to Omi Kunitama Shrine, held on the weekend before August 10 according to the lunar calendar. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{CulturalHeritageDB|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * **main hall** - Edo period (circa 1656). Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi Kunitama Shrine main hall] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Heiden and Haiden** - Edo period (1863). Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html Iwata City Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * **Yamamomo tree of Amamiko Shrine** - Designated on November 21, 2005. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi Kunitama Shrine] - Fuji no Kuni Cultural Resource Database (in Japanese). {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Important intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]]{{Sōja shrines}}[[category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Kenpeisha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Designated cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTŌmikunitama Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTŌmikunitama Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTŌmikunitama Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTŌmikunitama Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Images]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]]
