Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html Iwata City Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * **Yamamomo tree of Amamiko Shrine** - Designated on November 21, 2005. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi Kunitama Shrine] - Fuji no Kuni Cultural Resource Database (in Japanese). {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Important intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]]{{Sōja shrines}}[[category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Kenpeisha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Designated cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōmikunitama Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōmikunitama Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōmikunitama Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōmikunitama Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Images]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Omi Kunitama Shrine | image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = 2451 Mitsuke, Iwata City, Shizuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | Status = {{ill|式内社|en}} (Minor)
{{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}} | established = Unknown | Main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Rituals = [[July 13]], [[July 14]], [[July 15]] | map = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii (with [[Mitsuke Schoolhouse]] on the left)]] '''Omi Kunitama Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|淡海國玉神社}}, ''Ōmi Kunitama Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Iwata City|en}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|en}}, the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of Tōtōmi Province, and its modern shrine ranking was {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''[[Ōkuninushi]]''' - God of nation-building. === Enshrined deities === * **Mikogami Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]''' - Grandchild of [[Amaterasu]] and ancestral deity of the Imperial family. * '''[[Konohanasakuya-hime]]''' - Daughter of [[Ōyamatsumi]] and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * **Miso Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Kamo no Miso no Kami]]''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * **Sunawaka Wako Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Sunawaka Wako no Kami]]''' - Child of [[Takeminakata]] and deity of Mitsuke’s development. == History == The founding year of the shrine is unknown, but it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en}} as a {{ill|式内社|en}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the {{ill|Provincial capital|en}} of Tōtōmi Province, and Omi Kunitama Shrine was revered as the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}}. Situated in the center of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|en}}, the shrine stands next to the {{ill|Mitsuke Schoolhouse|en}} and the Iwata Library. In December 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri|en}} was designated an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. The shrine's main hall was designated as a cultural property by Iwata City in 2005 and by Shizuoka Prefecture in 2015. In 2016, the heiden (offering hall) and haiden (worship hall) were also designated as cultural properties by the city. == Grounds == * **main hall**: Built during the Edo period (circa 1656), it is a three-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated a tangible cultural property by Shizuoka Prefecture. * **Heiden (Offering Hall)**: Built in 1863 during the Edo period, it is an [[irimoya-zukuri]] structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated a tangible cultural property by Iwata City. * **Haiden (Worship Hall)**: Also built in 1863, it features an irimoya-zukuri style with a projecting roof and measures 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated a tangible cultural property by Iwata City. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|main hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * **Amamiko Shrine** - Dedicated to two deities of the {{ill|Amamiko no Kami|en}}. * **Raisan Shrine** - Enshrines the deities of {{ill|豊雷神社|en}}, {{ill|豊雷売命神社|en}}, and {{ill|生雷命神社|en}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Shrine File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Amamiko Shrine’s yamamomo tree (designated as a natural monument by Iwata City) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Shrine == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * **Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri**A festival involving the procession of the deity of {{ill|Yanaibime Shrine|en}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) to Omi Kunitama Shrine, held on the weekend before August 10 according to the lunar calendar. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{CulturalHeritageDB|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * **main hall** - Edo period (circa 1656). Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi Kunitama Shrine main hall] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Heiden and Haiden** - Edo period (1863). Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html Iwata City Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * **Yamamomo tree of Amamiko Shrine** - Designated on November 21, 2005. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi Kunitama Shrine] - Fuji no Kuni Cultural Resource Database (in Japanese). {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Important intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]]{{Sōja shrines}}[[category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Kenpeisha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Designated cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Omikunitama Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Omikunitama Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Omikunitama Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Omikunitama Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Images]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]]

Former {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}} | established = Unknown | Main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Rituals = [[July 13]], [[July 14]], [[July 15]] | map = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii (with [[Mitsuke Schoolhouse]] on the left)]] '''Omi Kunitama Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|淡海國玉神社}}, ''Ōmi Kunitama Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Iwata City|en}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|en}}, the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of Tōtōmi Province, and its modern shrine ranking was {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''[[Ōkuninushi]]''' - God of nation-building. === Enshrined deities === * **Mikogami Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]''' - Grandchild of [[Amaterasu]] and ancestral deity of the Imperial family. * '''[[Konohanasakuya-hime]]''' - Daughter of [[Ōyamatsumi]] and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * **Miso Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Kamo no Miso no Kami]]''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * **Sunawaka Wako Shrine (式内社)**: * '''[[Sunawaka Wako no Kami]]''' - Child of [[Takeminakata]] and deity of Mitsuke’s development. == History == The founding year of the shrine is unknown, but it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en}} as a {{ill|式内社|en}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the {{ill|Provincial capital|en}} of Tōtōmi Province, and Omi Kunitama Shrine was revered as the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}}. Situated in the center of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|en}}, the shrine stands next to the {{ill|Mitsuke Schoolhouse|en}} and the Iwata Library. In December 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri|en}} was designated an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. The shrine's main hall was designated as a cultural property by Iwata City in 2005 and by Shizuoka Prefecture in 2015. In 2016, the heiden (offering hall) and haiden (worship hall) were also designated as cultural properties by the city. == Grounds == * **main hall**: Built during the Edo period (circa 1656), it is a three-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated a tangible cultural property by Shizuoka Prefecture. * **Heiden (Offering Hall)**: Built in 1863 during the Edo period, it is an [[irimoya-zukuri]] structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated a tangible cultural property by Iwata City. * **Haiden (Worship Hall)**: Also built in 1863, it features an irimoya-zukuri style with a projecting roof and measures 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated a tangible cultural property by Iwata City. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|main hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * **Amamiko Shrine** - Dedicated to two deities of the {{ill|Amamiko no Kami|en}}. * **Raisan Shrine** - Enshrines the deities of {{ill|豊雷神社|en}}, {{ill|豊雷売命神社|en}}, and {{ill|生雷命神社|en}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Shrine File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Amamiko Shrine’s yamamomo tree (designated as a natural monument by Iwata City) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Shrine == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Designated) === * **Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri**A festival involving the procession of the deity of {{ill|Yanaibime Shrine|en}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) to Omi Kunitama Shrine, held on the weekend before August 10 according to the lunar calendar. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{CulturalHeritageDB|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Hadaka Matsuri] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * **main hall** - Edo period (circa 1656). Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi Kunitama Shrine main hall] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Heiden and Haiden** - Edo period (1863). Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html Iwata City Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * **Yamamomo tree of Amamiko Shrine** - Designated on November 21, 2005. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi Kunitama Shrine] - Fuji no Kuni Cultural Resource Database (in Japanese). {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Important intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]]{{Sōja shrines}}[[category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Kenpeisha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Designated cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Omikunitama Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Omikunitama Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Omikunitama Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Omikunitama Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Images]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Omi Kunitama Shrine | image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = 2451 Mitsuke, Iwata City, Shizuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | Status = {{ill|式内社|en}} (Minor)

Former {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}} | established = Unknown | Main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Rituals = [[July 13]], [[July 14]], [[July 15]] | map = Japan Shizuoka }} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | Name = Omi-Kunitama Jinja | Image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}} Mitsuke 2451 | ISO = JP | Latitude = 34 | Lat_Min = 43 | Lat_Sec = 40.1 | NS = N | Longitude = 137 | Lon_Min = 51 | Lon_Sec = 25.7 | EW = E | Main_deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor)
{{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}} | Founded = Unknown | Main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alias = | Annual_festival = {{ill|July 13|ja|7月13日}}, {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}}, {{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日}} }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校}})}}]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Mitsuke, {{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}}. == Deities == === Main Deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社}}, and {{ill|Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi-Kunitama Jinja] - Fuji-no-Kuni Cultural Heritage Database {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Sōja]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[ja:淡海國玉神社]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Important intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]]{{Sōja shrines}}[[category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Kenpeisha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Designated cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Omikunitama Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Omikunitama Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Omikunitama Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Omikunitama Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Images]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:Kk]][[ja:淡海國玉神社]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}} Mitsuke 2451 | ISO = JP | Latitude = 34 | Lat_Min = 43 | Lat_Sec = 40.1 | NS = N | Longitude = 137 | Lon_Min = 51 | Lon_Sec = 25.7 | EW = E | Main_deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor)
{{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}} | Founded = Unknown | Main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alias = | Annual_festival = {{ill|July 13|ja|7月13日}}, {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}}, {{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日}} }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校}})}}]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Mitsuke, {{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}}. == Deities == === Main Deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社}}, and {{ill|Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi-Kunitama Jinja] - Fuji-no-Kuni Cultural Heritage Database {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Sōja]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[ja:淡海國玉神社]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Important intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]]{{Sōja shrines}}[[category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Kenpeisha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Designated cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Omikunitama Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Omikunitama Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Omikunitama Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Omikunitama Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Images]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:refreshing categories falsely categorized as uncategorized]] [[ja:淡海國玉神社]]

File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi-Kunitama Jinja] - {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Omi Kunitama Shrine | image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = 2451 Mitsuke, Iwata City, Shizuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | Status = {{ill|式内社|en}} (Minor)
{{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}} | established = Unknown | Main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Rituals = [[July 13]], [[July 14]], [[July 15]] | map = Japan Shizuoka }} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | Name = Omi-Kunitama Jinja | Image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}} Mitsuke 2451 | ISO = JP | Latitude = 34 | Lat_Min = 43 | Lat_Sec = 40.1 | NS = N | Longitude = 137 | Lon_Min = 51 | Lon_Sec = 25.7 | EW = E | Main_deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor)
{{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}} | Founded = Unknown | Main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alias = | Annual_festival = {{ill|July 13|ja|7月13日}}, {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}}, {{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日}} }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校}})}}]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Mitsuke, {{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}}. == Deities == === Main Deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社}}, and {{ill|Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi-Kunitama Jinja] - Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}} Mitsuke 2451 | ISO = JP | Latitude = 34 | Lat_Min = 43 | Lat_Sec = 40.1 | NS = N | Longitude = 137 | Lon_Min = 51 | Lon_Sec = 25.7 | EW = E | Main_deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor)
{{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}} | Founded = Unknown | Main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alias = | Annual_festival = {{ill|July 13|ja|7月13日}}, {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}}, {{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日}} }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校}})}}]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Mitsuke, {{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}}. == Deities == === Main Deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社}}, and {{ill|Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社}}, and {{ill|Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Omi Kunitama Shrine | image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = 2451 Mitsuke, Iwata City, Shizuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | Status = {{ill|式内社|en}} (Minor)
{{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}} | established = Unknown | Main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Rituals = [[July 13]], [[July 14]], [[July 15]] | map = Japan Shizuoka }} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Omi-Kunitama Jinja | image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}} Mitsuke 2451 | ISO = JP | Latitude = 34 | Lat_Min = 43 | Lat_Sec = 40.1 | NS = N | Longitude = 137 | Lon_Min = 51 | Lon_Sec = 25.7 | EW = E | Main_deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor)
{{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alias = | Annual_festival = {{ill|July 13|ja|7月13日}}, {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}}, {{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日}} }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校}})}}]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Mitsuke, {{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}}. == Deities == === Main Deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Omi Kunitama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = 2451 Mitsuke, Iwata City, Shizuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | Status = {{ill|式内社|en}} (Minor)
{{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}} | established = Unknown | Main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Rituals = [[July 13]], [[July 14]], [[July 15]] | map = Japan Shizuoka | name = Omi-Kunitama Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}} Mitsuke 2451 | ISO = JP | Latitude = 34 | Lat_Min = 43 | Lat_Sec = 40.1 | NS = N | Longitude = 137 | Lon_Min = 51 | Lon_Sec = 25.7 | EW = E | Main_deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor)
{{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alias = | Annual_festival = {{ill|July 13|ja|7月13日}}, {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}}, {{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日}} }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校}})}}]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Mitsuke, {{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}}. == Deities == === Main Deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社}}, and {{ill|Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi-Kunitama Jinja] - Fuji-no-Kuni Cultural Heritage Database {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}}. == Deities == === Main Deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社}}, and {{ill|Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi-Kunitama Jinja] - It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = 2451 Mitsuke, Iwata City, Shizuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | Status = {{ill|式内社|en}} (Minor)

Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}} Mitsuke 2451 | ISO = JP | Latitude = 34 | Lat_Min = 43 | Lat_Sec = 40.1 | NS = N | Longitude = 137 | Lon_Min = 51 | Lon_Sec = 25.7 | EW = E | Main_deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor)

Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alias = | Annual_festival = {{ill|July 13|ja|7月13日}}, {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}}, {{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日}} }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校}})}}]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Mitsuke, {{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}}. == Deities == === Main Deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Omi Kunitama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = 2451 Mitsuke, Iwata City, Shizuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | Status = {{ill|式内社|en}} (Minor)
{{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}} | established = Unknown | Main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Rituals = [[July 13]], [[July 14]], [[July 15]] | map = Japan Shizuoka | name = Omi-Kunitama Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}} Mitsuke 2451 | ISO = JP | Latitude = 34 | Lat_Min = 43 | Lat_Sec = 40.1 | NS = N | Longitude = 137 | Lon_Min = 51 | Lon_Sec = 25.7 | EW = E | Main_deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor)
{{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alias = | Annual_festival = {{ill|July 13|ja|7月13日}}, {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}}, {{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日}} }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校}})}}]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Mitsuke, {{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}}. == Deities == === Main Deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社}}, and {{ill|Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi-Kunitama Jinja] - Fuji-no-Kuni Cultural Heritage Database {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}}
