It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}}. == Deities == === Main Deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社}}, and {{ill|Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi-Kunitama Jinja] - Fuji-no-Kuni Cultural Heritage Database {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社}}, and {{ill|Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Omi Kunitama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = 2451 Mitsuke, Iwata City, Shizuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | Status = {{ill|式内社|en}} (Minor)
{{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}} | established = Unknown | Main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Rituals = [[July 13]], [[July 14]], [[July 15]] | map = Japan Shizuoka | name = Omi-Kunitama Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}} Mitsuke 2451 | ISO = JP | Latitude = 34 | Lat_Min = 43 | Lat_Sec = 40.1 | NS = N | Longitude = 137 | Lon_Min = 51 | Lon_Sec = 25.7 | EW = E | Main_deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor)
{{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alias = | Annual_festival = {{ill|July 13|ja|7月13日}}, {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}}, {{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日}} }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校}})}}]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Mitsuke, {{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社}}, and {{ill|Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = 2451 Mitsuke, Iwata City, Shizuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | Status = {{ill|式内社|en}} (Minor)

Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}} Mitsuke 2451 | ISO = JP | Latitude = 34 | Lat_Min = 43 | Lat_Sec = 40.1 | NS = N | Longitude = 137 | Lon_Min = 51 | Lon_Sec = 25.7 | EW = E | Main_deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor)
{{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alias = | Annual_festival = {{ill|July 13|ja|7月13日}}, {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}}, {{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日}} }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校}})}}]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Mitsuke, {{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社}}, and {{ill|Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake {{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神}} Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社}}, and {{ill|Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Omi Kunitama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = 2451 Mitsuke, Iwata City, Shizuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | Status = {{ill|式内社|en}} (Minor)
{{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}} | established = Unknown | Main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Rituals = [[July 13]], [[July 14]], [[July 15]] | map = Japan Shizuoka | name = Omi-Kunitama Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}} Mitsuke 2451 | ISO = JP | Latitude = 34 | Lat_Min = 43 | Lat_Sec = 40.1 | NS = N | Longitude = 137 | Lon_Min = 51 | Lon_Sec = 25.7 | EW = E | Main_deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor)
{{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alias = | Annual_festival = {{ill|July 13|ja|7月13日}}, {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}}, {{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日}} }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is the former [[:en:Mitsuke School|Mitsuke School]]}}]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Mitsuke, {{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake {{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神}} Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社}}, and {{ill|Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Omi Kunitama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = 2451 Mitsuke, Iwata City, Shizuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | Status = {{ill|式内社|en}} (Minor)
{{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}} | established = Unknown | Main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Rituals = [[July 13]], [[July 14]], [[July 15]] | map = Japan Shizuoka | name = Omi-Kunitama Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}} Mitsuke 2451 | ISO = JP | Latitude = 34 | Lat_Min = 43 | Lat_Sec = 40.1 | NS = N | Longitude = 137 | Lon_Min = 51 | Lon_Sec = 25.7 | EW = E | Main_deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor)
{{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alias = | Annual_festival = {{ill|July 13|ja|7月13日}}, {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}}, {{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日}} }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is the former [[:en:Mitsuke School|Mitsuke School]]}}]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Mitsuke, {{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake {{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神}} Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社}}, and {{ill|Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alias = | Annual_festival = {{ill|July 13|ja|7月13日}}, {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}}, {{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日}} }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is the former [[:en:Mitsuke School|Mitsuke School]]}}]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Mitsuke, {{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake {{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神}} Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社}}, and {{ill|Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社}}, and {{ill|Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}} Mitsuke 2451 | ISO = JP | Latitude = 34 | Lat_Min = 43 | Lat_Sec = 40.1 | NS = N | Longitude = 137 | Lon_Min = 51 | Lon_Sec = 25.7 | EW = E | Main_deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor)

Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alias = | Annual_festival = {{ill|July 13|ja|7月13日}}, {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}}, {{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日}} }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is the former [[:en:Mitsuke School|Mitsuke School]]}]]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Mitsuke, {{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake {{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神}} Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Omi Kunitama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = 2451 Mitsuke, Iwata City, Shizuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | Status = {{ill|式内社|en}} (Minor)
{{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}} | established = Unknown | Main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Rituals = [[July 13]], [[July 14]], [[July 15]] | map = Japan Shizuoka | name = Omi-Kunitama Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}} Mitsuke 2451 | ISO = JP | Latitude = 34 | Lat_Min = 43 | Lat_Sec = 40.1 | NS = N | Longitude = 137 | Lon_Min = 51 | Lon_Sec = 25.7 | EW = E | Main_deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor)
{{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alias = | Annual_festival = {{ill|July 13|ja|7月13日}}, {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}}, {{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日}} }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is the former [[:en:Mitsuke School|Mitsuke School]]}]]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Mitsuke, {{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake {{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神}} Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社}}, and {{ill|Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Omi Kunitama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = 2451 Mitsuke, Iwata City, Shizuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | Status = {{ill|式内社|en}} (Minor)
{{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Kenpeisha|en}} | established = Unknown | Main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Rituals = [[July 13]], [[July 14]], [[July 15]] | map = Japan Shizuoka | name = Omi-Kunitama Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}} Mitsuke 2451 | ISO = JP | Latitude = 34 | Lat_Min = 43 | Lat_Sec = 40.1 | NS = N | Longitude = 137 | Lon_Min = 51 | Lon_Sec = 25.7 | EW = E | Main_deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor)
{{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alias = | Annual_festival = {{ill|July 13|ja|7月13日}}, {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}}, {{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日}} }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is the former [[:en:Mitsuke School|Mitsuke School]]}]]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Mitsuke, {{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake {{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神}} Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社}}, and {{ill|Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi-Kunitama Jinja] - Fuji-no-Kuni Cultural Heritage Database {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alias = | Annual_festival = {{ill|July 13|ja|7月13日}}, {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}}, {{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日}} }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is the former [[:en:Mitsuke School|Mitsuke School]]}]]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Mitsuke, {{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake {{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神}} Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社}}, and {{ill|Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake {{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神}} Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社}}, and {{ill|Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} *
