{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Omi Kunitama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = 2451 Mitsuke, Iwata City, Shizuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | Status = {{ill|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|en|qq=draft}} (Minor)
{{ill|Sōja|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Kenpeisha|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | established = Unknown | Main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Rituals = [[July 13]], [[July 14]], [[July 15]] | map = Japan Shizuoka | name = Omi-Kunitama Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mitsuke 2451 | ISO = JP | Latitude = 34 | Lat_Min = 43 | Lat_Sec = 40.1 | NS = N | Longitude = 137 | Lon_Min = 51 | Lon_Sec = 25.7 | EW = E | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor)
{{ill|Sōja|ja|総社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Alias = | Annual_festival = {{ill|July 13|ja|7月13日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is the former [[:en:Mitsuke School|Mitsuke School]]}]]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Mitsuke, {{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake {{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi-Kunitama Jinja] - Fuji-no-Kuni Cultural Heritage Database {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Omikunitama Shrine}} {{citation needed|Omikunitama Shrine}} {{citation needed|Omikunitama Shrine}} {{citation needed|Omikunitama Shrine}} {{draft categories| }} {{Sōja shrines}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Sōja]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Important intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Kenpeisha]] [[Category:Designated cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[Category:Ōmikunitama Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:淡海國玉神社]] [[sn:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[en:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[ja:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|淡海國玉神社|version=102047454|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mitsuke 2451 | ISO = JP | Latitude = 34 | Lat_Min = 43 | Lat_Sec = 40.1 | NS = N | Longitude = 137 | Lon_Min = 51 | Lon_Sec = 25.7 | EW = E | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor)
{{ill|Sōja|ja|総社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Alias = | Annual_festival = {{ill|July 13|ja|7月13日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is the former [[:en:Mitsuke School|Mitsuke School]]}]]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Mitsuke, {{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake {{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi-Kunitama Jinja] - Fuji-no-Kuni Cultural Heritage Database {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Omikunitama Shrine}} {{citation needed|Omikunitama Shrine}} {{citation needed|Omikunitama Shrine}} {{citation needed|Omikunitama Shrine}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|淡海國玉神社|version=102047454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Sōja]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Important intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Kenpeisha]] [[Category:Designated cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[Category:Ōmikunitama Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:淡海國玉神社]] [[sn:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[en:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[ja:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]]

It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake {{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi-Kunitama Jinja] - Fuji-no-Kuni Cultural Heritage Database {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|淡海國玉神社|version=102047454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Sōja]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Important intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Kenpeisha]] [[Category:Designated cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[Category:Ōmikunitama Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:淡海國玉神社]] [[sn:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[en:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[ja:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]]

The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mitsuke 2451 | ISO = JP | Latitude = 34 | Lat_Min = 43 | Lat_Sec = 40.1 | NS = N | Longitude = 137 | Lon_Min = 51 | Lon_Sec = 25.7 | EW = E | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor)

Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Alias = | Annual_festival = {{ill|July 13|ja|7月13日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is the former [[:en:Mitsuke School|Mitsuke School]]}]]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Mitsuke, {{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake {{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi-Kunitama Jinja] - Fuji-no-Kuni Cultural Heritage Database {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|淡海國玉神社|version=102047454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Sōja]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Important intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Kenpeisha]] [[Category:Designated cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[Category:Ōmikunitama Shrine]] }} [[ja:淡海國玉神社]] [[sn:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[en:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[ja:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Omi Kunitama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = 2451 Mitsuke, Iwata City, Shizuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | Status = {{ill|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|en|qq=draft}} (Minor)
{{ill|Sōja|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Kenpeisha|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | established = Unknown | Main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Rituals = [[July 13]], [[July 14]], [[July 15]] | map = Japan Shizuoka | name = Omi-Kunitama Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mitsuke 2451 | ISO = JP | Latitude = 34 | Lat_Min = 43 | Lat_Sec = 40.1 | NS = N | Longitude = 137 | Lon_Min = 51 | Lon_Sec = 25.7 | EW = E | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor)
{{ill|Sōja|ja|総社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Alias = | Annual_festival = {{ill|July 13|ja|7月13日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is the former [[:en:Mitsuke School|Mitsuke School]]}]]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Mitsuke, {{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake {{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi-Kunitama Jinja] - Fuji-no-Kuni Cultural Heritage Database {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|淡海國玉神社|version=102047454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Sōja]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Important intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Kenpeisha]] [[Category:Designated cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[Category:Ōmikunitama Shrine]] }} [[ja:淡海國玉神社]] [[sn:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[en:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[ja:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mitsuke 2451 | ISO = JP | Latitude = 34 | Lat_Min = 43 | Lat_Sec = 40.1 | NS = N | Longitude = 137 | Lon_Min = 51 | Lon_Sec = 25.7 | EW = E | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor)
{{ill|Sōja|ja|総社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Alias = | Annual_festival = {{ill|July 13|ja|7月13日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is the former [[:en:Mitsuke School|Mitsuke School]]}]]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Mitsuke, {{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake {{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi-Kunitama Jinja] - Fuji-no-Kuni Cultural Heritage Database {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|淡海國玉神社|version=102047454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Sōja]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Important intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Kenpeisha]] [[Category:Designated cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[Category:Ōmikunitama Shrine]] }} [[ja:淡海國玉神社]] [[sn:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[en:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[ja:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]]

Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtōmi Province|qq=}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Mitsuke School|qq=}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke-juku|qq=}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|qq=}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwata|qq=}} Mitsuke 2451 | ISO = JP | Latitude = 34 | Lat_Min = 43 | Lat_Sec = 40.1 | NS = N | Longitude = 137 | Lon_Min = 51 | Lon_Sec = 25.7 | EW = E | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} | Shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor)
{{ill|Sōja|ja|総社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtōmi Province|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine (Japan)|qq=}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} | Alias = | Annual_festival = {{ill|July 13|ja|7月13日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 13|qq=}}, {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 14|qq=}}, {{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 15|qq=}} }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is the former [[:en:Mitsuke School|Mitsuke School]]}]]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in Mitsuke, {{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwata|qq=}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka Prefecture|qq=}}. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtōmi Province|qq=}}. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine (Japan)|qq=}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial House of Japan|qq=}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Mikogami|qq=}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake {{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikogami|qq=}} Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunawake Mikogami|qq=}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtōmi Province|qq=}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Mitsuke School|qq=}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke-juku|qq=}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|qq=}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|qq=}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokaminari Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokaminarime Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikazuchi Shrine|qq=}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|qq=}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanahime Shrine|qq=}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi-Kunitama Jinja] - Fuji-no-Kuni Cultural Heritage Database {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|淡海國玉神社|version=102047454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Sōja]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Important intangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Designated cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[Category:Ōmikunitama Shrine]] }} [[ja:淡海國玉神社]] [[sn:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[en:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[ja:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]]

A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi-Kunitama Jinja] - Fuji-no-Kuni Cultural Heritage Database {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|淡海國玉神社|version=102047454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Sōja]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Designated cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[Category:Ōmikunitama Shrine]] }} [[ja:淡海國玉神社]] [[sn:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[en:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[ja:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]]

Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = 2451 Mitsuke, Iwata City, Shizuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | Status = {{ill|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/式内社|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Minor)
{{ill|Sōja|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtōmi Province|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Kenpeisha|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenpeisha|qq=}} | established = Unknown | Main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Rituals = [[July 13]], [[July 14]], [[July 15]] | map = Japan Shizuoka | name = Omi-Kunitama Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwata|qq=}} Mitsuke 2451 | ISO = JP | Latitude = 34 | Lat_Min = 43 | Lat_Sec = 40.1 | NS = N | Longitude = 137 | Lon_Min = 51 | Lon_Sec = 25.7 | EW = E | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} | Shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor)
{{ill|Sōja|ja|総社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtōmi Province|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine (Japan)|qq=}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} | Alias = | Annual_festival = {{ill|July 13|ja|7月13日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 13|qq=}}, {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 14|qq=}}, {{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 15|qq=}} }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is the former [[:en:Mitsuke School|Mitsuke School]]}]]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in Mitsuke, {{ill|Iwata|ja|磐田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwata|qq=}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka Prefecture|qq=}}. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtōmi Province|qq=}}. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine (Japan)|qq=}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial House of Japan|qq=}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Mikogami|qq=}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake {{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikogami|qq=}} Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunawake Mikogami|qq=}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtōmi Province|qq=}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Former Mitsuke School|ja|旧見付学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Mitsuke School|qq=}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke-juku|qq=}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|qq=}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|qq=}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokaminari Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokaminarime Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Raijin Shrine|lt=Raijin Shrine|lt=Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikazuchi Shrine|qq=}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|qq=}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanahime Shrine|qq=}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi-Kunitama Jinja] - Fuji-no-Kuni Cultural Heritage Database {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|淡海國玉神社|version=102047454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Sōja]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Designated cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[Category:Ōmikunitama Shrine]] }} [[ja:淡海國玉神社]] [[sn:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[en:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[ja:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]]
