{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Omi Kunitama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = 2451 Mitsuke, Iwata City, Shizuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | Status = {{ill|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/式内社|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Minor)
{{ill|Sōja|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtōmi Province|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Kenpeisha|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenpeisha|qq=}} | established = Unknown | Main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Rituals = [[July 13]], [[July 14]], [[July 15]] | map = Japan Shizuoka | name = Omi-Kunitama Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata]] Mitsuke 2451 | ISO = JP | Latitude = 34 | Lat_Min = 43 | Lat_Sec = 40.1 | NS = N | Longitude = 137 | Lon_Min = 51 | Lon_Sec = 25.7 | EW = E | deity = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor)
[[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja]] of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine (Japan)|qq=}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_building_style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alias = | Annual_festival = [[:en:July 13|July 13]], [[:en:July 14|July 14]], [[:en:July 15|July 15]] }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is the former [[:en:Mitsuke School|Mitsuke School]]}]]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Mitsuke, [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and served as the [[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja]] of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine (Japan)|qq=}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} and ancestral deity of the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]]. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Mikogami|qq=}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake {{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikogami|qq=}} Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunawake Mikogami|qq=}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|1=Takeminakata|2=ja|3=タケミナカタ|lt=Takeminakata|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]], and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its [[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja]]. The shrine is located near the [[:en:Mitsuke School|Former Mitsuke School]] and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke-juku|qq=}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|qq=}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|qq=}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokaminari Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokaminarime Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Raijin Shrine|lt=Raijin Shrine|lt=Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikazuchi Shrine|qq=}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|qq=}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanahime Shrine|qq=}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi-Kunitama Jinja] - Fuji-no-Kuni Cultural Heritage Database {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|淡海國玉神社|version=102047454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Sōja]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Designated cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:淡海國玉神社]] [[sn:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[ja:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:静岡県の旧県社]] {{Commons category|Omi-kunitama-jinja}}

[[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja]] of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_building_style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alias = | Annual_festival = [[:en:July 13|July 13]], [[:en:July 14|July 14]], [[:en:July 15|July 15]] }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is the former [[:en:Mitsuke School|Mitsuke School]]}]]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Mitsuke, [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} and served as the [[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja]] of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]. The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and ancestral deity of the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]]. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Mikogami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake {{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikogami|qq=}} Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunawake Mikogami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' - Offspring of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]], and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its [[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja]]. The shrine is located near the [[:en:Mitsuke School|Former Mitsuke School]] and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke-juku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見附宿}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokaminari Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokaminarime Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Raijin Shrine|lt=Raijin Shrine|lt=Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikazuchi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanahime Shrine|qq=}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi-Kunitama Jinja] - Fuji-no-Kuni Cultural Heritage Database {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|淡海國玉神社|version=102047454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Sōja]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Designated cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:淡海國玉神社]] [[sn:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[ja:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:静岡県の旧県社]] {{Commons category|Omi-kunitama-jinja}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Omi Kunitama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = 2451 Mitsuke, Iwata City, Shizuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | Status = {{ill|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/式内社|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (Minor)
{{ill|Sōja|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtōmi Province|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Kenpeisha|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenpeisha|qq=}} | established = Unknown | Main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Rituals = [[July 13]], [[July 14]], [[July 15]] | map = Japan Shizuoka | name = Omi-Kunitama Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata]] Mitsuke 2451 | ISO = JP | Latitude = 34 | Lat_Min = 43 | Lat_Sec = 40.1 | NS = N | Longitude = 137 | Lon_Min = 51 | Lon_Sec = 25.7 | EW = E | deity = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (minor)
[[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja]] of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_building_style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alias = | Annual_festival = [[:en:July 13|July 13]], [[:en:July 14|July 14]], [[:en:July 15|July 15]] }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is the former [[:en:Mitsuke School|Mitsuke School]]}]]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Mitsuke, [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} and served as the [[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja]] of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and ancestral deity of the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]]. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Mikogami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake {{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikogami|qq=}} Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunawake Mikogami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' - Offspring of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]], and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its [[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja]]. The shrine is located near the [[:en:Mitsuke School|Former Mitsuke School]] and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke-juku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見附宿}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokaminari Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokaminarime Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Raijin Shrine|lt=Raijin Shrine|lt=Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikazuchi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanahime Shrine|qq=}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi-Kunitama Jinja] - Fuji-no-Kuni Cultural Heritage Database {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|淡海國玉神社|version=102047454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Sōja]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:淡海國玉神社]] [[sn:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[ja:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:静岡県の旧県社]] {{Commons category|Omi-kunitama-jinja}}

Offspring of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]], and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its [[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja]]. The shrine is located near the [[:en:Mitsuke School|Former Mitsuke School]] and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke-juku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見附宿}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokaminari Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokaminarime Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Raijin Shrine|lt=Raijin Shrine|lt=Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikazuchi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanahime Shrine|qq=}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi-Kunitama Jinja] - Fuji-no-Kuni Cultural Heritage Database {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|淡海國玉神社|version=102047454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Sōja]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:淡海國玉神社]] [[sn:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[ja:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:静岡県の旧県社]] {{Commons category|Omi-kunitama-jinja}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata]] Mitsuke 2451 | ISO = JP | Latitude = 34 | Lat_Min = 43 | Lat_Sec = 40.1 | NS = N | Longitude = 137 | Lon_Min = 51 | Lon_Sec = 25.7 | EW = E | deity = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (minor)
[[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja]] of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_building_style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alias = | Annual_festival = [[:en:July 13|July 13]], [[:en:July 14|July 14]], [[:en:July 15|July 15]] }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is the former [[:en:Mitsuke School|Mitsuke School]]}]]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Mitsuke, [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} and served as the [[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja]] of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and ancestral deity of the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]]. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Mikogami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake {{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikogami|qq=}} Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunawake Mikogami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' - Offspring of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]], and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its [[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja]]. The shrine is located near the [[:en:Mitsuke School|Former Mitsuke School]] and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke-juku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見附宿}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi-Kunitama Jinja] - Fuji-no-Kuni Cultural Heritage Database {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|淡海國玉神社|version=102047454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Sōja]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:淡海國玉神社]] [[sn:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[ja:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Omi-kunitama-jinja}}

The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and ancestral deity of the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]]. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Mikogami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake {{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikogami|qq=}} Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunawake Mikogami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' - Offspring of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]], and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its [[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja]]. The shrine is located near the [[:en:Mitsuke School|Former Mitsuke School]] and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke-juku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見附宿}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokaminari Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokaminarime Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Raijin Shrine|lt=Raijin Shrine|lt=Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikazuchi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanahime Shrine|qq=}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi-Kunitama Jinja] - Fuji-no-Kuni Cultural Heritage Database {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|淡海國玉神社|version=102047454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:淡海國玉神社]] [[sn:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[ja:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Omi-kunitama-jinja}}

The shrine is located near the [[:en:Mitsuke School|Former Mitsuke School]] and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke-juku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見附宿}}. {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata]] Mitsuke 2451 | ISO = JP | Latitude = 34 | Lat_Min = 43 | Lat_Sec = 40.1 | NS = N | Longitude = 137 | Lon_Min = 51 | Lon_Sec = 25.7 | EW = E | deity = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] | Shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
[[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja]] of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_building_style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alias = | Annual_festival = [[:en:July 13|July 13]], [[:en:July 14|July 14]], [[:en:July 15|July 15]] }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is the former [[:en:Mitsuke School|Mitsuke School]]}]]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Mitsuke, [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and served as the [[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja]] of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and ancestral deity of the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]]. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Mikogami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake {{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikogami|qq=}} Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunawake Mikogami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' - Offspring of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]], and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its [[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja]]. The shrine is located near the [[:en:Mitsuke School|Former Mitsuke School]] and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke-juku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見附宿}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokaminari Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokaminarime Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Raijin Shrine|lt=Raijin Shrine|lt=Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikazuchi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanahime Shrine|qq=}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi-Kunitama Jinja] - Fuji-no-Kuni Cultural Heritage Database {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|淡海國玉神社|version=102047454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:淡海國玉神社]] [[sn:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[ja:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Omi-kunitama-jinja}}
