Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} | Alias = | Annual_festival = {{ill|July 13|en|July 13|WD=Q2687|lt=July 13}}, {{ill|July 14|en|July 14|WD=Q2691|lt=July 14}}, {{ill|July 15|en|July 15|WD=Q2686|lt=July 15}} }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is the former {{ill|Mitsuke School|en|Mitsuke School|WD=Q48748988|lt=Mitsuke School}}}]]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Mitsuke, {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|en|Iwata, Shizuoka|WD=Q386651|lt=Iwata}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province}}. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Mikogami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake {{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikogami|qq=}} Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunawake Mikogami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Mitsuke School|en|Mitsuke School|WD=Q48748988|lt=Former Mitsuke School}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke-juku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見附宿|1=Mitsuke-juku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q782144}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokaminari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Raijin Shrine|lt=Raijin Shrine|lt=Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikazuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanahime Shrine|1=Yanahime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65498694}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province}}. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Mikogami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake {{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikogami|qq=}} Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunawake Mikogami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Mitsuke School|en|Mitsuke School|WD=Q48748988|lt=Former Mitsuke School}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke-juku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見附宿|1=Mitsuke-juku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q782144}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|en|Iwata, Shizuoka|WD=Q386651|lt=Iwata}} Mitsuke 2451 | ISO = JP | Latitude = 34 | Lat_Min = 43 | Lat_Sec = 40.1 | NS = N | Longitude = 137 | Lon_Min = 51 | Lon_Sec = 25.7 | EW = E | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}} | Shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
{{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} | Alias = | Annual_festival = {{ill|July 13|en|July 13|WD=Q2687|lt=July 13}}, {{ill|July 14|en|July 14|WD=Q2691|lt=July 14}}, {{ill|July 15|en|July 15|WD=Q2686|lt=July 15}} }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is the former {{ill|Mitsuke School|en|Mitsuke School|WD=Q48748988|lt=Mitsuke School}}}]]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Mitsuke, {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|en|Iwata, Shizuoka|WD=Q386651|lt=Iwata}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province}}. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Mikogami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake {{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikogami|qq=}} Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunawake Mikogami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Mitsuke School|en|Mitsuke School|WD=Q48748988|lt=Former Mitsuke School}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke-juku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見附宿|1=Mitsuke-juku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q782144}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokaminari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Raijin Shrine|lt=Raijin Shrine|lt=Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikazuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanahime Shrine|1=Yanahime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65498694}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi-Kunitama Jinja] - Fuji-no-Kuni Cultural Heritage Database {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|淡海國玉神社|version=102047454|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:淡海國玉神社]] [[ja:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] {{Commons category|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q43594535}} [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Sixth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

{{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja shrine}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province|1=Tōtōmi Province}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Alias = | Annual_festival = {{ill|July 13|en|July 13|WD=Q2687|lt=July 13|1=July 13}}, {{ill|July 14|en|July 14|WD=Q2691|lt=July 14|1=July 14}}, {{ill|July 15|en|July 15|WD=Q2686|lt=July 15|1=July 15}} }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is the former {{ill|Mitsuke School|en|Mitsuke School|WD=Q48748988|lt=Mitsuke School|1=Mitsuke School}}}]]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Mitsuke, {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|en|Iwata, Shizuoka|WD=Q386651|lt=Iwata|1=Iwata, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja shrine}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province|1=Tōtōmi Province}}. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan|1=Imperial House of Japan}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Mikogami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake {{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikogami|qq=}} Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunawake Mikogami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province|1=Tōtōmi Province}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja shrine}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Mitsuke School|en|Mitsuke School|WD=Q48748988|lt=Former Mitsuke School|1=Mitsuke School}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke-juku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見附宿|1=Mitsuke-juku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q782144}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokaminari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Raijin Shrine|lt=Raijin Shrine|lt=Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikazuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanahime Shrine|1=Yanahime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65498694}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi-Kunitama Jinja] - Fuji-no-Kuni Cultural Heritage Database {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|淡海國玉神社|version=102047454|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:淡海國玉神社]] [[ja:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] {{Commons category|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q43594535}} [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Sixth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|en|Iwata, Shizuoka|WD=Q386651|lt=Iwata|1=Iwata, Shizuoka}} Mitsuke 2451 | ISO = JP | Latitude = 34 | Lat_Min = 43 | Lat_Sec = 40.1 | NS = N | Longitude = 137 | Lon_Min = 51 | Lon_Sec = 25.7 | EW = E | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}} | Shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
{{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja shrine}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province|1=Tōtōmi Province}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Alias = | Annual_festival = {{ill|July 13|en|July 13|WD=Q2687|lt=July 13|1=July 13}}, {{ill|July 14|en|July 14|WD=Q2691|lt=July 14|1=July 14}}, {{ill|July 15|en|July 15|WD=Q2686|lt=July 15|1=July 15}} }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is the former {{ill|Mitsuke School|en|Mitsuke School|WD=Q48748988|lt=Mitsuke School|1=Mitsuke School}}}]]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Mitsuke, {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|en|Iwata, Shizuoka|WD=Q386651|lt=Iwata|1=Iwata, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja shrine}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province|1=Tōtōmi Province}}. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan|1=Imperial House of Japan}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Mikogami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake {{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikogami|qq=}} Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunawake Mikogami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province|1=Tōtōmi Province}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja shrine}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Mitsuke School|en|Mitsuke School|WD=Q48748988|lt=Former Mitsuke School|1=Mitsuke School}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke-juku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見附宿|1=Mitsuke-juku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q782144}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokaminari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Raijin Shrine|lt=Raijin Shrine|lt=Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikazuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanahime Shrine|1=Yanahime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65498694}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi-Kunitama Jinja] - Fuji-no-Kuni Cultural Heritage Database {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|淡海國玉神社|version=102047454|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:淡海國玉神社]] [[ja:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] {{Commons category|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q43594535}} [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Sixth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

{{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja shrine}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province|1=Tōtōmi Province}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Alias = | Annual_festival = {{ill|July 13|en|July 13|WD=Q2687|lt=July 13|1=July 13}}, {{ill|July 14|en|July 14|WD=Q2691|lt=July 14|1=July 14}}, {{ill|July 15|en|July 15|WD=Q2686|lt=July 15|1=July 15}} }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is the former {{ill|Mitsuke School|en|Mitsuke School|WD=Q48748988|lt=Mitsuke School|1=Mitsuke School}}}]]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Mitsuke, {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|en|Iwata, Shizuoka|WD=Q386651|lt=Iwata|1=Iwata, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja shrine}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province|1=Tōtōmi Province}}. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan|1=Imperial House of Japan}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Mikogami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake {{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikogami|qq=}} Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunawake Mikogami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province|1=Tōtōmi Province}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja shrine}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Mitsuke School|en|Mitsuke School|WD=Q48748988|lt=Former Mitsuke School|1=Mitsuke School}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke-juku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見附宿|1=Mitsuke-juku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q782144}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokaminari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Raijin Shrine|lt=Raijin Shrine|lt=Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikazuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanahime Shrine|1=Yanahime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65498694}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja shrine}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province|1=Tōtōmi Province}}. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan|1=Imperial House of Japan}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Mikogami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake {{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikogami|qq=}} Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunawake Mikogami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province|1=Tōtōmi Province}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja shrine}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Mitsuke School|en|Mitsuke School|WD=Q48748988|lt=Former Mitsuke School|1=Mitsuke School}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke-juku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見附宿|1=Mitsuke-juku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q782144}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokaminari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Raijin Shrine|lt=Raijin Shrine|lt=Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikazuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanahime Shrine|1=Yanahime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65498694}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi-Kunitama Jinja] - Fuji-no-Kuni Cultural Heritage Database {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|淡海國玉神社|version=102047454|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:淡海國玉神社]] [[ja:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] {{Commons category|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q43594535}} [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Sixth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

[[co:commons:Category:Omi-kunitama-jinja]][[ja:淡海國玉神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Omi-kunitama-jinja]][[ja:淡海國玉神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Omi-kunitama-jinja]][[ja:淡海國玉神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Omi Kunitama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = 2451 Mitsuke, Iwata City, Shizuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}} | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] | Status = {{ill|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/式内社|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor)
{{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtōmi Province|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Kenpeisha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenpeisha|qq=}} | established = Unknown | Main_hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Rituals = [[July 13]], [[July 14]], [[July 15]] | map = Japan Shizuoka | name = Omi-Kunitama Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omi kunitama jinja shaden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|43|40.1|N|137|51|25.7|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|en|Iwata, Shizuoka|WD=Q386651|lt=Iwata|1=Iwata, Shizuoka}} Mitsuke 2451 | ISO = JP | Latitude = 34 | Lat_Min = 43 | Lat_Sec = 40.1 | NS = N | Longitude = 137 | Lon_Min = 51 | Lon_Sec = 25.7 | EW = E | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}} | Shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
{{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja shrine}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province|1=Tōtōmi Province}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Alias = | Annual_festival = {{ill|July 13|en|July 13|WD=Q2687|lt=July 13|1=July 13}}, {{ill|July 14|en|July 14|WD=Q2691|lt=July 14|1=July 14}}, {{ill|July 15|en|July 15|WD=Q2686|lt=July 15|1=July 15}} }} [[File:Omi-kunitama-jinja entrance torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii (to the left is the former {{ill|Mitsuke School|en|Mitsuke School|WD=Q48748988|lt=Mitsuke School|1=Mitsuke School}}}]]] '''Omi-Kunitama Jinja''' (淡海國玉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Mitsuke, {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|en|Iwata, Shizuoka|WD=Q386651|lt=Iwata|1=Iwata, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and served as the {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja shrine}} of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province|1=Tōtōmi Province}}. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == === Main deity === * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}''' - The god of nation-building. === Enshrined Deities === * Mikogami Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊々杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}''' - Grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and ancestral deity of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan|1=Imperial House of Japan}}. ** '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}''' - Daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} and wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto. * Miso Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Kamo no Mikogami|ja|鴨御祖神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Mikogami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' - Ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. * Sunawake {{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikogami|qq=}} Jinja (式内社) ** '''{{ill|Sunawake Mikogami|ja|須波若彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunawake Mikogami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' - Offspring of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}}, revered as the deity who developed Mitsuke. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. Mitsuke (Iwata) was the location of the provincial capital of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province|1=Tōtōmi Province}}, and Omi-Kunitama Jinja served as its {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja shrine}}. The shrine is located near the {{ill|Mitsuke School|en|Mitsuke School|WD=Q48748988|lt=Former Mitsuke School|1=Mitsuke School}} and the Iwata Bunko library in the central part of the former {{ill|Mitsuke-juku|ja|見付宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke-juku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見附宿|1=Mitsuke-juku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q782144}}. On December 27, 2000, the {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} by the Japanese government. The main shrine building was designated as an Iwata City-designated Cultural Property in 2005 and as a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated Cultural Property on December 8, 2015. On March 28, 2016, the Heiden (offering hall) and Haiden (worship hall) were designated as Iwata City-designated Cultural Properties. == Grounds == * Main Shrine - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. A three-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with a frontage of 5 meters and a depth of 5.14 meters. Designated as a Shizuoka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. * Heiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with a frontage and depth of 4.54 meters. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. * Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. An Irimoya-zukuri structure with an attached portico, measuring 10 meters in frontage and 6.36 meters in depth. Designated as an Iwata City Tangible Cultural Property. File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Shrine File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine Hall File:Omi-kunitama-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Middle Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Amamiko Jinja - Listed as the {{ill|Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja|天御子神社二座|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiko Jinja (Two Shrines)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * Raisan Jinja - Includes the {{ill|Toyokaminari Shrine|ja|豊雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokaminari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja|豊雷売命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokaminarime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Raijin Shrine|lt=Raijin Shrine|lt=Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|生雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikazuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. File:Amamiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amamiko Jinja File:Amamiko-jinja, yamamomo.jpg|Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja (designated as an Iwata City Natural Monument) File:Raisan-jinja, torii.jpg|Raisan Jinja == Cultural Properties == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National) === * {{ill|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja|見付天神裸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}This festival centers on the deity of {{ill|Yanahime Shrine|ja|矢奈比売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanahime Shrine|1=Yanahime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65498694}} (Mitsuke Tenjin) visiting Omi-Kunitama Jinja. It is held on the weekend closest to the 10th day of the 8th lunar month. - Designated on December 27, 2000.{{Cultural Heritage Database|302|738|Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival}}[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002052.html Mitsuke Tenjin Naked Festival] (Iwata City website). === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Main Shrine (including five ridge tags) - Constructed around Meireki 3 (1656) during the Edo period. Designated on December 8, 2015.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kenshitei/1002069.html Omi-Kunitama Jinja Main Shrine, including five ridge tags] (Iwata City website). === Iwata City Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Heiden and Haiden - Constructed in Bunkyū 3 (1863) during the Edo period. Designated on March 28, 2016.[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/bunkazainitsuite/1002039.html City-designated Cultural Properties] (Iwata City website). * Natural Monument ** Yamamomo Tree at Amamiko Jinja - Located in the grounds of Amamiko Jinja. Designated on November 21, 2005. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} * [http://www.fujinokunibunkashigen.net/resouce/main.php?search=category&mode=detail&article=1420 Omi-Kunitama Jinja] - Fuji-no-Kuni Cultural Heritage Database {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|淡海國玉神社|version=102047454|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:淡海國玉神社]] [[ja:Ōmi Kunitama Shrine]] {{Commons category|Omi-kunitama-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q43594535}} [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Sixth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
